Scenario.title: Phishing-Based Attack on Healthcare Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple This scenario describes a simple yet effective cyber attack targeting a healthcare organization. The attack begins with a phishing campaign to gain initial access, followed by privilege escalation using Mimikatz, and culminates in the disruption of SCADA systems controlling critical infrastructure.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disruption of critical infrastructure for geopolitical purposes

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 164 Name: Mobile Phishing Description: An adversary targets mobile phone users with a phishing attack for the purpose of soliciting account passwords or sensitive information from the user.
stage.description: The attack commences with a phishing email campaign targeting employees of the healthcare organization. The phishing emails contain malicious links designed to steal employee credentials, leveraging known vulnerabilities in web-based communication platforms.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 467 Name: Cross Site Identification Description: An attacker harvests identifying information about a victim via an active session that the victim's browser has with a social networking site.
stage.description: Once initial access is gained, the attacker deploys Mimikatz to escalate privileges within the compromised system. This tool is used to extract and manipulate authentication tokens, enabling further lateral movement within the network.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522 Name: Malicious Hardware Component Replacement Description: An adversary replaces legitimate hardware in the system with faulty counterfeit or tampered hardware in the supply chain distribution channel, with purpose of causing malicious disruption or allowing for additional compromise.
stage.description: The final phase involves the manipulation of SCADA systems managing critical healthcare infrastructure. By exploiting vulnerabilities in these systems, the attacker disrupts operations, leading to potential service outages and significant financial and operational damage.

Scenario.summary: This scenario outlines a straightforward yet impactful cyber attack on a healthcare organization. The attack leverages phishing, privilege escalation, and SCADA system tampering to achieve its objectives, demonstrating the potential consequences of failing to secure critical infrastructure against determined adversaries.
